channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Why Trump faces toughest House GOP test yet on budget blueprint
item.description: Wednesday’s looming House vote on the Senate’s framework for advancing Trump’s legislative agenda is shaping up to be one of the president’s toughest tests yet on Capitol Hill, as hardline Republicans dismiss the White House’s public — and private — entreaties to line up behind the measure that will
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Duffy slams MTA over 'fact check' on anti-Israel mob's Grand Central takeover
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy and New York's MTA had a heated exchange over whether anti-Israel protests in Grand Central Station caused service disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judges temporarily block 5 Venezuelans' deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: Federal judges in New York and Texas temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting five Venezuelan men it alleges are gang members by using the Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration appeals court decision ordering AP journalists back into White House events
item.description: The Trump administration said Wednesday it would appeal a federal court decision that ordered it to re-admit Associated Press journalists to White House events on First Amendment grounds.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC lets immigration officials open an office at Rikers jail, a priority for Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams ' administration is allowing federal immigration officials to operate at Rikers Island to work on gang and drug-related criminal investigations in the city's largest lockup.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 124 dead after roof collapse at Dominican club, officials scramble to ID victims
item.description: Rescue crews from Puerto Rico and Israel arrived Wednesday to help with the search. Officials said crews used sonar to detect what could be one person still breathing under the rubble.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Are dire wolves de-extinct? Genetically engineered pups spark controversy
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wild fish tale: Kayaker takes on 140-pound tuna in the Outer Banks
item.description: A man in the Outer Banks is riding a high after a true tuna tale that, for him, is the fishing catch of a lifetime.
item.dc:creator: Madison Pearman, NewsNation

item.title: Kidnapping and conspiracy charges are dropped against college students in ‘Catch a Predator’ fad
item.description: A judge has dismissed conspiracy and kidnapping charges against five Massachusetts college students who were accused of plotting to lure a man to their campus through a dating app and then seizing him as part of a “Catch a Predator” trend on social media.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: YouTuber and entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson, called the tariffs a "nail in the coffin" for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: The world's most famous YouTuber slams Trump's tariff war
item.description: Entrepreneur and YouTube star MrBeast slammed President Trump’s “brutal” tariffs in a post on the social platform X on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Federal government questioned former Columbia University president over campus antisemitism
item.description: Former Columbia University interim President Katrina Armstrong struggled to answer questions from Department of Health and Human Services investigators over campus antisemitism issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump tariffs, including massive levies on China, go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump's latest round of sweeping reciprocal tariffs went into effect early Wednesday, including a 104 percent import tax on China.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: New tariffs take effect
item.description: President Donald Trump's latest round of tariffs is now in effect and concerns from Capitol Hill are growing louder, even among Republican lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Brandt, Fox 5

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, NewsNation

item.title: Trump botching tariff messaging because he lacks empathy: Cenk Uygur
item.description: "Why is [Trump] blowing it? Partly because he lacks empathy," Uygur told NewsNation's Leland Vittert on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft fires employees who protested its support for Israel at 50th anniversary event
item.description: Two Microsoft employees were fired for protests condemning the company’s support for Israel at a 50th anniversary event last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Feds launch task force to investigate fraud and corruption in Southern California homeless funds
item.description: U.S. Attorney Bill Essayli announced a new task force to investigate alleged fraud and waste involving billions of dollars allocated to address homelessness in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees, which was said to have been planted by Jackson in the 19th century, was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: ICE agent impersonations spike in wake of immigration crackdown
item.description: Impersonatorsposing as immigration agents are spreading fear among migrants already uncertain about their future in the country.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: Small plane skids off runway at Oregon airport, ends up in bay
item.description: A small private plane attempting to land at an Oregon airport skidded off the runway and into a body of water, leaving 5 people injured.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Foran, Fox 5

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Scholarships for child care are drying up. Now families are paying the price
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — For parents who need to work but can't afford the steep cost of child care, federally funded scholarships can be a lifeline. Delivered through state child care assistance programs, the scholarships can mean the difference between a parent working full time — or not at all.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal magistrate weighs arguments in Idaho news groups' execution access lawsuit
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — A federal magistrate judge is expected to hear arguments Tuesday morning in a lawsuit brought by three news organizations that say Idaho prison officials are unconstitutionally hiding parts of lethal injection executions from public view.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clem Burke, multifaceted drummer of iconic rock group Blondie, has died
item.description: Clem Burke, whose versatile drumming propelled the iconic rock group Blondie during its decades performing everything from new-wave punk to disco-infused tunes, has died. He was 70. The band said in a statement on its website Monday that he died from cancer but no additional details were provided.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to deport Venezuelans under wartime law, but only after judges' review
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday allowed the Trump administration to use an 18th century wartime law to deport Venezuelan migrants, but said they must get a court hearing before they are taken from the United States.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Epic Universe opens a portal to the future of theme parks
item.description: As Universal Orlando Resort gears up for the highly anticipated May 22 opening of Epic Universe, it’s clear the resort is entering a new era - one that could redefine what a theme park can offer families.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WGN 9

item.title: Maine sues the Trump administration over funding freeze after dispute over transgender athletes
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — Maine officials sued the administration of President Donald Trump on Monday to try to stop the government from freezing federal money in the wake of a dispute over transgender athletes in sports.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators have signed onto a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump planning military parade through DC for 79th birthday
item.description: The Trump administration is planning a June 14 military parade through the streets of Washington, D.C., to mark the president's 79th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Rising rivers threaten US South and Midwest after dayslong torrent of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Kentucky (AP) — Rivers rose and flooding worsened Sunday across the sodden U.S. South and Midwest, threatening communities already badly damaged by days of heavy rain and wind that killed at least 18 people.
item.dc:creator: JON CHERRY, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, Fox 5
